title: Athlete's Foot Treatment: Top 7 Herbal Creams for Fast Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Terpinen-4-ol, commonly known as tea tree oil, is good for athlete's foot because it has antifungal properties that help to kill the fungus that causes the infection, providing relief from itching and discomfort.
Linalool is good for athlete's foot because it has antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing the infection, reducing redness, itching, and discomfort.
Cineole is good for athlete's foot because it has antifungal properties that help to kill the fungus causing the infection and soothe the irritated skin.
Catechins, also known as thyme, have antifungal properties that help to combat the fungus causing athlete's foot by reducing its growth and killing its cells.

Polyphenols, also known as Thyme, have antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections, such as athlete's foot, by reducing the growth of the fungus and promoting a healthy environment for the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream, also known as tea tree oil cream, is a popular treatment for athlete's foot. This is because it has natural antifungal properties that help to fight the fungus causing the infection. The cream is absorbed into the skin, where it kills the fungus that's causing the problem, such as athlete's foot. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling. Tea tree oil is a natural ingredient that's been used for centuries to treat skin infections and wounds. When applied to the affected area, it helps to soothe and calm the skin, making it feel more comfortable and reducing the risk of further infection. Additionally, Melaleuca alternifolia cream is non-irritating and gentle on the skin, making it suitable for people with sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus vulgaris herbal creams that help with athlete's foot, first gather the necessary ingredients. You will need dried thyme leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and a few drops of tea tree oil. Start by mixing 2 tablespoons of dried thyme leaves with 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for about 10 minutes, or until the thyme leaves are well combined with the oil. Next, add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the mixture, and stir until the wax and butter are fully melted. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly, then add a few drops of tea tree oil to give the cream its antifungal properties. Transfer the mixture to a glass jar and let it cool completely before using it as a cream to help soothe and heal athlete's foot. Apply a thin layer of the cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day, and cover with a bandage or sock to lock in the moisture and allow the cream to work its magic.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for athlete's foot

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins have antifungal properties that help fight the fungus causing athlete's foot, reducing its severity and promoting healthy skin.
Cineole is good for athlete's foot because it has antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing the infection, reducing itching and discomfort.
Catechins are good for athlete's foot because they have antifungal properties that can help kill the fungi that cause the infection and soothe the itchy, irritated skin.
Polyphenols are good for athlete's foot because they have antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing the infection, reducing itching and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams can help with athlete's foot because it has special properties that fight the fungus causing the infection. The active ingredient in eucalyptus globulus, called cineole, has antibacterial and antifungal properties. This means it can kill the fungus and bacteria that cause athlete's foot, reducing redness, itching, and burning sensations. Eucalyptus globulus creams can also help to dry out the affected area, preventing the fungus from spreading and making it harder for new infections to develop. When applied directly to the affected area, the cream can soothe and calm the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus vulgaris herbal cream for athlete's foot, start by infusing dried thyme leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Fill a clean glass jar with 1 part dried thyme leaves and 2 parts carrier oil, making sure the leaves are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the thyme leaves. Next, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter, and a bit of water to achieve the desired consistency. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl, stirring until the beeswax or shea butter is fully melted and the cream is smooth. Finally, pour the cream into a clean glass jar and let it cool before using it to treat athlete's foot - apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day, and watch for improvement in the fungal infection.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for athlete's foot

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins have antifungal properties that can help to kill the fungus causing athlete's foot, thereby providing relief from symptoms such as itching and discomfort.
Tannins are good for athlete's foot because they have antifungal properties that help fight and prevent fungal infections that cause the condition.
Flavonoids are good for athlete's foot because they have strong antifungal properties that can help kill the fungus causing the infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a good treatment for athlete's foot because it has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. This means it can reduce redness, itching, and swelling caused by athlete's foot. The cream also helps to heal the skin and prevent further infection. Calendula officinalis has natural antiseptic properties that kill fungi and bacteria that cause athlete's foot. It also helps to dry out the affected area, which prevents the spread of infection. When applied to the affected skin, the cream can help to soothe and calm the area, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, Calendula officinalis cream is often used to promote wound healing and tissue repair, which is beneficial for athlete's foot that has caused cracked and broken skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus vulgaris herbal cream that helps with athlete's foot, you'll need a few ingredients. First, dry the thyme leaves in a low-temperature oven or through the air-drying method, making sure not to overheat them. Once the thyme leaves are completely dry, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix 2 tablespoons of the thyme powder with 1/4 cup of coconut oil or olive oil in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture for a few minutes until the oil is fully infused with the thyme's medicinal properties. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool down. After the mixture has cooled, add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel to it, then stir well until the beeswax is fully dissolved. Finally, pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool completely before using it as a cream to help with athlete's foot symptoms. Remember to store the cream in a cool, dry place to maintain its effectiveness.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for athlete's foot

Anthraquinones are good for athlete's foot because they have antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing the infection, thereby providing relief from itching and discomfort.
Salicylic acid is good for athlete's foot because it helps to soften and remove dead skin cells, which can help to get rid of the fungus that causes the infection.
Tannins are good for athlete's foot because they have antifungal properties that can help kill the fungus causing the infection and prevent its spread.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera cream is a great treatment for athlete's foot. It helps to soothe and calm the skin, which is irritated and itchy due to the infection. The cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. Aloe vera also has antifungal properties, which fight the fungus that causes athlete's foot. When applied to the affected area, the cream helps to dry out the fungal infection, preventing it from spreading further. This makes it easier for the skin to heal and recover. Aloe vera is also a natural and gentle remedy, making it suitable for people with sensitive skin. It can be applied to the feet several times a day to help manage symptoms and prevent further infection.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Thymus vulgaris herbal cream that helps with athlete's foot, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried thyme leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and a beeswax or shea butter to thicken the cream. Begin by making a thyme infusion by mixing 1 tablespoon of dried thyme leaves with 8 ounces of hot water. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Next, combine the thyme infusion with 8 ounces of carrier oil in a small saucepan and heat it gently over low heat, stirring occasionally. Once the mixture has cooled slightly, add 2-3 tablespoons of beeswax or shea butter and stir until it's fully incorporated and the mixture has thickened into a smooth cream. Transfer the cream to a clean container and let it cool completely before using it on affected areas of the skin. Apply the cream 2-3 times a day to help soothe and treat athlete's foot, and enjoy the benefits of thyme's antifungal and antibacterial properties.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for athlete's foot

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Aloe-emodin is good for athlete's foot because it has antifungal properties that can help stop the growth of fungi that cause this condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a popular natural remedy for athlete's foot. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and itching caused by the infection. Aloe vera contains compounds called glycoproteins and polysaccharides that can soothe and calm the skin. When applied to the affected area, it can create a protective barrier that prevents moisture from entering and making the infection worse. Aloe barbadensis also has antifungal properties that can help control the growth of fungi that cause athlete's foot. This can help to speed up the healing process and reduce the risk of further infection. Additionally, aloe vera is gentle and non-irritating, making it a suitable option for sensitive skin. It can be used as a standalone treatment or in combination with other anti-fungal creams to enhance their effectiveness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus vulgaris herbal cream for athlete's foot, first, you need to make a herbal infusion by combining 1 cup of fresh thyme leaves with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant bowl. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Next, in a separate bowl, mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil or shea butter with 1/4 cup of beeswax pellets in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the mixture is melted, remove it from the heat and let it cool slightly before adding 2 tablespoons of the herbal infusion to the mixture. Stir well to combine and pour the mixture into a glass jar. Let the cream cool and harden completely before use. When applying the cream, gently massage a small amount onto the affected area 2-3 times a day to help soothe and prevent fungal infections.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for athlete's foot

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as lavender oil, is a natural ingredient often used in creams to treat athlete's foot. This is because the oil has antifungal properties, which means it can help stop the growth of fungi that cause the infection. The creams typically contain a combination of lavender oil and other ingredients, such as tea tree oil, that also have antifungal properties. When applied to the affected area, the cream helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing itching and discomfort. The antifungal properties of lavender oil also help to kill the fungi, allowing the skin to heal naturally. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to keep the skin hydrated and healthy, which is especially important for areas that are prone to dryness and cracking, like the feet. By applying the cream regularly, people with athlete's foot can help to control the infection and prevent it from spreading.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Thymus vulgaris herbal cream that helps with athlete's foot, you will need to gather a few simple ingredients. Start by infusing dried thyme leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for several weeks. This can be done by placing the dried thyme in a clean glass jar and covering it with the carrier oil. Let it sit in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar occasionally, until the oil has taken on the scent and color of the thyme. Next, mix this infused oil with a moisturizing base like shea butter or beeswax, which will help to soothe and protect the skin. You can also add some tea tree oil, which is known for its antifungal properties, to enhance the cream's effectiveness against athlete's foot. Finally, whip the mixture until it's smooth and creamy, then transfer it to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to keep it fresh. When you need to use it, simply take a small amount and apply it to the affected area, repeating as needed until the symptoms of athlete's foot have cleared up.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for athlete's foot

Roserminic acid is good for athlete's foot because it has antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing the infection, thereby reducing symptoms and promoting healing of the affected area.
Caryophyllene is good for athlete's foot because it has antifungal properties that can help kill the fungus causing the infection, reducing itching and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris cream is a natural remedy that can help treat athlete's foot. It contains thymol, a chemical that fights fungus and bacteria that cause infections. This cream helps to kill the fungus that causes athlete's foot, making it a good choice for people who prefer natural treatments. Thymus vulgaris cream is also antifungal and antibacterial, which means it can prevent new infections from forming. It can also soothe and calm the affected area, reducing itching and discomfort. Thymus vulgaris cream is often used to treat athlete's foot because it is gentle on the skin and does not have harsh chemicals like some other treatments.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus vulgaris herbal creams that help with athlete's foot, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you will need to dry and grind the Thyme leaves into a fine powder. To do this, place the fresh Thyme leaves in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for a few hours, or until they are completely dry. Once dry, simply place the leaves in a coffee grinder or a spice grinder and grind them into a fine powder. Next, you will need to combine the ground Thyme powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut oil or olive oil, in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the Thyme essence and the mixture has cooled to room temperature. Then, you will need to add a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, to the mixture and heat it gently until the beeswax or shea butter has melted. Finally, pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, your Thymus vulgaris herbal cream is ready to use. To apply it, simply massage a small amount onto the affected area and reapply as needed.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for athlete's foot

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a good solution for athlete's foot because they often contain natural ingredients that help to soothe and heal the affected area. Two herbs commonly found in these creams are 'Melaleuca alternifolia' (tea tree oil) and 'Eucalyptus globulus' (eucalyptus oil). Tea tree oil has antifungal properties that can help to kill the fungus causing the infection, while eucalyptus oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce itching and discomfort. When left untreated, athlete's foot can lead to further complications such as cracked skin, bacterial infections, and even nerve damage. If not addressed, it can also affect daily life, causing discomfort and pain while walking or engaging in physical activities. By using herbal creams containing these ingredients, individuals can help to alleviate symptoms, prevent the spread of the infection, and improve their overall quality of life. By addressing the issue, you can reduce the risk of complications, regain confidence in your feet, and enjoy activities without discomfort or pain.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal creams to remedy athlete's foot includes Melaleuca alternifolia (tea tree oil), Calendula officinalis, and Aloe barbadensis. Melaleuca alternifolia has antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing athlete's foot. Calendula officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and itching caused by the infection. Aloe barbadensis has soothing properties that calm the skin and speed up the healing process. This combination works well because the antifungal properties of Melaleuca alternifolia help eliminate the fungus, while the anti-inflammatory properties of Calendula officinalis reduce symptoms, and the soothing properties of Aloe barbadensis promote healing and comfort. The cream can be applied directly to the affected area, ideally after bathing or showering, to help manage and treat athlete's foot.
supplementary_avoid: If you have athlete's foot, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. Tea tree oil, while commonly used for skin issues, can be too strong for sensitive skin and may cause irritation. Some herbal creams containing chamomile may not be effective against fungal infections and may even soothe the fungus, allowing it to grow further. Sage, while known for its antifungal properties, can be too drying and may strip the skin of its natural oils, making it more vulnerable to infection. Also, avoid creams containing witch hazel, as it can sting and cause discomfort, which may worsen the condition. Additionally, avoid creams containing bergamot, as it can cause skin sensitivity and may react with other ingredients, making the condition worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Apple cider vinegar has been used as a natural remedy for athlete's foot. The acidity in the vinegar helps to kill the fungus that causes the infection. To use it, mix equal parts of apple cider vinegar and water in a foot bath and soak the feet for 15-20 minutes, twice a week. The acidity in the vinegar also helps to soften and dry out the skin, making it harder for the fungus to grow.  Tea tree oil is another natural remedy for athlete's foot. It has antifungal properties that help to kill the fungus. To use it, mix a few drops of tea tree oil with water and apply it to the affected area with a cotton swab. The oil can also be added to a foot bath to help soothe and dry out the skin.  Oregano oil is also used to treat athlete's foot due to its antifungal properties. It can be applied directly to the affected area with a cotton swab or added to a foot bath to help kill the fungus. The oil can also help to reduce the itching and burning associated with athlete's foot.  Baking soda is used to dry out the skin and help to kill the fungus that causes athlete's foot. To use it, mix baking soda with water to form a paste and apply it to the affected area. The paste can also be used to soak the feet in a foot bath.  Coconut oil has antifungal properties that help to kill the fungus that causes athlete's foot. To use it, apply coconut oil to the affected area with a cotton swab. The oil can also be used to moisturize the skin and help to soothe any itching or burning associated with the infection.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with athlete's foot often have other skin problems. One common issue is fungal toenail infections. This is because the same fungus that causes athlete's foot can also infect toenails. Medicinal herbs like tea tree oil and oregano oil have antifungal properties that can help treat fungal toenail infections. Tea tree oil can be applied directly to the infected nail, while oregano oil can be added to a foot soak to help kill the fungus.  Another common issue is fungal nail infections in the fingernails, known as onychomycosis. This is a fungal infection of the fingernails that can be treated with antifungal herbs like garlic and turmeric. Garlic has antifungal properties that can help kill the fungus, while turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling.  People with athlete's foot are also more likely to experience skin irritation and eczema. This is because the same fungus that causes athlete's foot can also cause skin irritation and inflammation. Medicinal herbs like aloe vera and chamomile can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and inflammation.  Skin cracks and fissures are also common in people with athlete's foot. This is because the fungus that causes athlete's foot can dry out the skin, causing cracks and fissures. Medicinal herbs like calendula and vitamin E oil can help moisturize and protect the skin, preventing cracks and fissures.  Fungal infections of the skin, like ringworm and jock itch, can also be treated with medicinal herbs like tea tree oil and oregano oil. These herbs have antifungal properties that can help kill the fungus, reducing symptoms and preventing the infection from spreading.
